commonIssues:
Discrepancies between the survey number in the sale deed and the actual revenue records are common, causing immediate application rejection.

How to fix:

  • Verify FMB (Field Measurement Book) sketches via the Tamil Nilam portal before drafting the deed. Tamil Nilam

Land records often do not reflect recent subdivisions, mergers, or resurveys, leading to problems in locating the correct property in the system.

How to fix:

  • Apply for sub-division or Patta transfer online via the e-Sevai portal. e-Sevai Portal

Failure to trace the ownership history (Mother Deed) or missing links in the chain of ownership.

How to fix:

  • Obtain a certified copy (CC) of the parent document from TNREGINET. TNREGINET

Failing to obtain necessary approvals (like DTCP, CMDA) or NOCs before starting the registration process.

How to fix:

  • Ensure DTCP/CMDA approved layouts are used. Apply for approval if missing. CMDA Portal

Applications are automatically rejected if there are outstanding land taxes (Kist), water cess, or property taxes, which is frequent in inherited properties.

How to fix:

  • Pay outstanding property tax and water tax via local corporation portals or e-Sevai.

Long wait times for Patta transfer approval after the sale deed is registered.

How to fix:

  • Track Patta transfer status on the Tamil Nilam portal. Contact Tahsildar if delayed over 30 days.

Even after paying, the data may not update immediately on the TNREGINET portal, particularly for older, non-digitized records.

How to fix:

  • Visit the SRO for manual verification if the record was registered before digitization.

Failure of the physical land layout to match official map records (FMB), leading to disputes with neighbors.

How to fix:

  • Apply for a land survey through the e-Sevai portal by paying the prescribed fee.

Errors in spelling or owner names between the sale deed and the Patta.

How to fix:

  • Submit a name correction application to the Tahsildar along with a gazette notification or affidavit.

Instances where multiple Pattas are issued for the same survey number.

How to fix:

  • File a complaint with the District Registrar to cancel fraudulent registrations under Section 77A of the Registration Act.
